title: abc9: fix SCC issues
body:
Fix #2530 -- specifically `(* abc9_box, lib_whitebox *)` cells that can be bypassed using a combinatorial-only box are no longer bypassed if they form part of a combinatorial loop (based on analysing connectivity only, not if it such a condition can be reached or will cause undefined behaviour -- such are the testcases from #2530 which occurs when the output of an async flop (which causes a bypass cell to capture async behaviour) fans back into to its async set/reset input).

Improve SCC handling overall. This is achieved by no longer marking combinatorial loops with `(* abc9_keep *)` and letting `write_xaiger` turn them into `inout`s -- this doesn't work if a box output drives a PO which cannot be turned into an `inout` -- so now use an explicit `$__ABC9_SCC_BREAKER` cell. 

Also tightens up some `abc9` testing.

CC: @Ravenslofty 

Please squash merge.
